description: Product or branded element is held, worn, or used by a person in a candid or semi-candid lifestyle moment — the brand is embedded in a human action rather than displayed as an object. Focus is on the human moment, product is secondary.
structural_recipe: Mid or close shot of hands, face, or full body engaged in an activity where the product naturally appears. Lighting: available or warm-filled. Setting: real-world (not studio). Model's attention is on the activity, not the camera — candid gaze preferred over direct-to-lens.
why_it_works: Creates social proof through normalisation — the product exists in a real person's moment, not a brand photoshoot. Distinct from lifestyle_environment_integration in that the human action is the anchor, not the environment.
transplantation_caveats: Requires culturally appropriate gender presentation and attire.
Direct camera eye contact shifts this into model_centered_frontal_portrait — keep gaze off-camera for authentic 'embed' feel.
Works best when the human action is relatable and locally specific (pouring Saudi coffee, applying kohl, selecting abaya fabric).

provenance.source: corpus_synthesis_474_obs — 9 observed uses across all sectors
